meta.title: REDSL · Refactoring $10 per ticket
meta.description: Code refactoring per ticket for SaaS teams of 1-15 developers. $10 per ticket, no subscription.

hero.kicker: Refactoring SaaS for teams 1–15 devs · Poland
hero.headline:
Your team builds features.
ReDSL makes sure the code doesn't fall apart.
hero.lede: Working with LLMs, you've probably noticed something familiar — code gets written faster, but regression starts growing. This isn't a tool error. It's the effect of local changes in a global system. ReDSL works where LLMs lack full context — and stabilizes code parallel to development.
hero.bullet1: LLMs speed up coding but don't control quality over time
hero.bullet2: ReDSL analyzes the entire repo and reduces tech debt via small, measurable PRs
hero.bullet3: Team builds features while the system simplifies itself — without 'cleanup sprints'

pain.label: 01 · Problem
pain.title:
LLMs generate code faster,
but the system becomes more complex.
pain.without_1: duplicates appear in different files (LLM doesn't see the whole)
pain.without_2: functions grow because 'it works, don't touch'
pain.without_3: dependencies get complicated — effect of local changes
pain.without_4: regression grows. deploy becomes stressful. code fights you.
pain.with_1: analysis of entire repo — not just new code
pain.with_2: small, measurable PRs fix tech debt before it becomes critical
pain.with_3: system stabilizes itself parallel to development
pain.with_4: no one loses a week on cleanup — team builds features
pain.punchline: The team can finally build — instead of fearing what happens when something moves.

why.label: 02 · Why LLM + ReDSL
why.title_1: LLM sees locally, ReDSL sees globally
why.desc_1: AI generates code based on prompt — doesn't know the whole system. ReDSL analyzes entire repo and detects duplicates, bad dependencies, growing complexity.
why.desc_2: Small, measurable PRs fix tech debt before it becomes critical. Deploy stops being stressful. Changes become predictable.
why.desc_3: Quality grows parallel with the product — team builds features while system simplifies itself. No 'refactoring sprints'.

process.label: 03 · How it works
process.title: Five steps. One email per month.
process.step1_title: Setup 30 min, one-time SaaS · PHP · redsl
process.step1_desc: Read access + create-PR, NDA, CI and branch. One short call.
process.step2_title: Repo analysis up to 24h, $0 SaaS · PHP · redsl
process.step2_desc: Full code scan — duplicates, complexity, dependencies. List of tickets with before/after metrics and price.
process.step3_title: Selection as much as you want
process.step3_desc: One email: 'do 1, 3, 7' or 'everything under $50'. Decision is yours.
process.step4_title: Pull Requests in 24–48h SaaS · PHP · redsl
process.step4_desc: Separate PR per ticket. Before/after metrics, green CI. If CI fails — our problem.
process.step5_title: Invoice once per month SaaS · PHP · redsl
process.step5_desc: Only for merged PRs. Rejected with feedback = $0. No response 14 days = auto-close.

pricing.label: 04 · Pricing
pricing.title: Two categories. That's it.
pricing.found_desc: Detected by analysis: duplicates, complexity, coupling.
pricing.made_desc: You report pain, we locate, quote and fix.

contact.label: 05 · Contact
contact.title: Two questions, one day response.
contact.subtitle: We don't do sales calls. Tell us what hurts, we'll tell you if we can help.
contact.form_placeholder: Using LLMs? What hurts in your code? What stack? How many people?
contact.form_micro: We respond within one business day. No auto-responder.
contact.sidebar_for_desc: Polish companies, team 1–15 devs, code lives 2+ years, using (or planning) LLMs, Python / JS / TS / Go / Rust.
contact.sidebar_not_desc: Teams 50+, enterprise, ISO 27001 / SOC 2, ML projects.
contact.sidebar_quote: Less debt. Less regression. Faster features. In practice: 20 PRs monthly, each with before/after metrics. For $40.

footer.copyright: Built in one night, maintained by one person.

proposals.title: Refactoring Proposals
proposals.hint: Each ticket: $10 (up to 500 LOC, merged PRs)
proposals.summary_note: Payment after PR merge. NDA signed before scan.
proposals.success: Selected %d tickets. Total: $%d. Confirmation sent to email.

client.title: Client Panel
client.subtitle: Here you will see your scan status, refactoring proposals and payment history.
client.logged_in: Successfully logged in via GitHub as
client.step2: We will run an automatic scan of 3 of your repos (within 24h)
client.step3: You will receive a refactoring proposals report at
client.step4: You choose which proposals to accept (price: $2-20/ticket)
client.step5: We create PRs, you approve and merge to main
